title: solo-suite/gate-evidence-v1
description: One machine-readable record backing one gate category verdict: EITHER a verified evidence record created through the canonical record_evidence.py workflow (policy-validated command, captured exit code, git-derived HEAD + committed-tree digest, hashed artifact) OR a not-applicable record created through its canonical --not-applicable workflow (git-derived commit, validated matrix cell, mandatory-category rejection, generated timestamps) and justified against the applicability matrix. TRUST MODEL: these are SELF-ATTESTED LOCAL EVIDENCE records — unsigned JSON that makes honest workflows mechanically checkable; they are NOT cryptographic attestations. The required recorder field is a copyable format label, not proof of which process created a record, so a manually constructed record that satisfies every content check cannot be distinguished cryptographically from helper output. A trusted CI identity/signature is the upgrade path. The single supported workflow keeps .solo/gate-evidence/ untracked/gitignored; records tracked in HEAD are an unsupported state both tools refuse. The matrix (normative copy in plugins/gate/lib/gate_policy.py): design/frontend N/A only for api-service|library-package; backend/database N/A only for public-marketing-site|library-package; performance N/A only for library-package; seo/analytics N/A only for internal-application|api-service|library-package; product, architecture, security, testing, deployment, monitoring, documentation may NEVER be N/A. A gate must reject records that are expired, from a different HEAD or committed tree, or from a different environment or project.

naCategory.description: Categories that CAN be N/A under at least one profile. The seven mandatory categories are structurally excluded here.

commit.description: Full 40-hex HEAD derived by the recorder via `git rev-parse HEAD` — the checker re-derives HEAD itself and requires EXACT equality.

tree_digest.description: SHA-256 over the COMMITTED tree at HEAD (path + blob sha from `git ls-tree -r HEAD`, excluding .solo/gate-evidence/**) — derived from git objects, so mutating working-tree bytes cannot change it. The checker recomputes and requires equality.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC time the evidence was gathered.

command.description: Canonical display of the executed argv - MUST equal ' '.join(command_argv); the checker enforces equality.

command_argv.description: Exact argv executed by record_evidence.py (no shell). The checker RE-VALIDATES this against the shared per-category command policy in gate_policy.py.

exit_code.description: Exit code captured by the canonical record_evidence.py workflow. The checker rejects nonzero values and re-validates the command policy, but unsigned JSON cannot cryptographically prove which process captured the value.

artifact.description: Path (inside the project root) of the captured stdout/stderr artifact, optionally with #anchor.

artifact_sha256.description: SHA-256 digest of the artifact content at evidence time; the checker recomputes it.

expires.description: ISO-8601 UTC expiry; stale evidence is rejected.

recorder.description: Identifies the producing tool. This is self-attestation, not proof of origin.

command_id.description: Canonical policy-entry id the argv matched (e.g. 'pytest', 'verify-artifact product').

resolved_executable.description: ABSOLUTE path of argv[0] after canonical PATH resolution (gate_policy.resolve_executable). The recorder executes THIS path, never the bare token; the checker re-resolves argv[0] and requires equality, rejecting unresolvable or project-local executables.

recorder.description: Required format label for the canonical record_evidence.py --not-applicable workflow. A record missing it fails schema validation, but the string is copyable and does not prove process origin; this branch remains self-attested local evidence.

commit.description: Full 40-hex HEAD — must exactly equal the HEAD the checker derives.

reason.description: Substantive justification (>= 20 chars and >= 4 words; the checker also enforces the word count). One character fails.

matrix.description: The '<category>:<profile>' matrix cell this N/A claims; the checker verifies the cell is permitted.

profile_source.description: Where the project profile is recorded (e.g. .solo/project.md).

checked.description: What was actually inspected to conclude non-applicability.
